content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 22, 1992


Karen A. O'Haire
Staff Counsel
State Water Resources Control Board
Paul R. Bonderson Building
901 P Street
Sacramento, CA 93812-0100

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-566

Dear Ms. O'Haire:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Mr. Hank Abraham with respect to his responsibilities as
a member of the California Regional Water Quality Control Board
pursuant to the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

1.  May Mr. Abraham, a member of the California Regional
Water Quality Control Board, prepare drawings or submissions
regarding Timber Harvest Plans to be used by a client in
connection with a proceeding before the California Regional Water
Quality Control Board?

2.  To what extent is Mr. Abraham permitted to contact staff
of the California Regional Water Quality Control Board with regard
to the client's proceeding before the agency?

CONCLUSIONS

1.  Mr. Abraham may prepare drawings or submissions regarding
timber harvest plans to be used by a client in connection with a
proceeding before the California Regional Water Quality Control
Board.


_________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-91-566
Page 2


     2.  Mr. Abraham is prohibited from direct oral or written
contact with the California Regional Water Quality Control Board
with regard to the timber harvest plan except for necessary
contact with agency staff.  "Necessary contact" has been
interpreted to permit the official only to respond to questions
from the staff of the agency concerning the submissions or plans.

                              FACTS

     Mr. Abraham is a board member of the California Regional
Water Quality Control Board (the "board"), Central Valley Region,
and a registered professional forester licensed by the California
Department of Forestry and Fire Protection.

     According to the information provided by Mr. Abraham, to
practice as a professional forester in California persons must be
licensed by the State Board of Forestry.  One function Mr. Abraham
performs as a professional forester is the preparation of timber
harvest plans (the "plan"), required by statute.2  The plan may
only be completed by a registered professional forester and must
comply with the forest practice rules of the State of California
in its development, submission, and procedures of inter-agency
review and pre-harvest inspection.

     The plan generally requires considerable experience in on-
site studies and engineering work.  In preparing the plan, the
professional forester uses his knowledge of silviculture, forest
soils, and other influences (such as fire control erosion and
water quality).  In addition, the professional forester must be
able to perform actual measurements of the land and timber stands,
locate and layout access roads and landings and other appurtenant
facilities.

     The pre-harvest inspection is held on the site of the plan.
During the pre-harvest inspection, questions regarding exact
intent and proposed means of road construction and timber harvest
methods and layout are discussed and answered.  Where more than
nominal damage is expected, measures to minimize and mitigate such
damage, if not already included in the plan by the professional
forester, are suggested and discussed to a conclusion by those
attending.

                              ANALYSIS

     Participation in a Governmental Decision

     Section 87100 of the Act provides:

          No public official at any level of state or
local government shall make, participate in making

_______________________
2    Requirements concerning the "timber-harvest plans" are in
Public Resources Code Sections 4581, et seq.

Our File No. A-91-566
Page 3


or in any way attempt to use his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

As a member of the California Regional Water Quality Control
Board, Mr. Abraham is a "public official" as defined in the Act.
(Section 82048.) Thus, he may not make, participate in making or
in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he has a financial interest.

Section 87103 provides that Mr. Abraham has an economic
interest in any decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable,
material financial effect on: (1) Mr. Abraham; (2) his immediate
family; (3) any business entity in which he has an investment
worth $1,000 or more, or in which he is a director, officer,
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management;
(4) any real property in which he has an interest worth $1,000 or
more; (5) and any source of $250 or more in income and gifts.

You have asked whether Mr. Abraham may prepare drawings or
submissions regarding timber harvest plans to be used by a client
in connection with a proceeding before the California Regional
Water Quality Control Board. Clearly, as discussed above,
Mr. Abraham would not be able to make, participate in making or in
any way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision relating to his own plans or his client's
project. (See, Nelson Advice Letter, No. I-91-443.)

However, Regulation 18700.1(b) provides:

[A]n official is not attempting to use his or
her official position to influence a governmental
decision ... if the official:

* * *

(4) Prepares drawings or submissions of an
architectural, engineering or similar nature to be
used by a client in connection with a proceeding
before any agency. However, this provision applies
only if the official has no other direct oral or
written contact with the agency with regard to the
client's proceeding before the agency except for
necessary contact with agency staff concerning the
processing or evaluation of the drawings or
submissions prepared by the official.

(Emphasis added.)

Our File No. A-91-566
Page 4


     The basis for the exception was set forth in the staff
memorandum submitted to the Commission in connection with the
exception. The memorandum stated:

          The cities were basically concerned about
     limitations on the professional activities of
     planning commissioners and city councilmembers who
     are architects and engineers. There was general
     agreement that planning commissioner/architects
     should not actually represent clients before the
     planning commission. It was also agreed that such
     architects and engineers should be able to prepare
     the drawings and plans for clients, even though the
     drawings and the plans would have to be submitted
     to the planning commission. There seemed to be
     general consensus among the city attorneys that
     this exception is necessary in order to allow the
     local architects and engineers to continue to
     practice their profession when they act as planning
     commissioners or councilmembers

     We see no reason to distinguish timber harvest plans or
professional foresters from the of this exception. The plan as
required in Public Resources Code Section 4582 appears to be the
type of technical plan that the exception was intended to
encompass. Public Resources Code Section 4582 provides:

          The timber harvesting plan shall be filed with
     the department in writing by a person who owns,
     leases, or otherwise controls or operates on all or
     any portion timberland and who plans to harvest the
     timber thereon. If the person who files the plan
     is not the owner of the timberland, the person
     filing the plan shall notify the timberland owner
     by certified mail that the plan has been submitted
     and shall certify that mailing to the department.
     The plan shall be a public record and shall include
     all of the following information:

          (a) The name and address of the timber
     owner.

          (b) The name and address of the timber
     operator.

          (c) A description of the land on which
     the work is proposed to be done, including a
     United States Geological Survey quadrangle map
     or equivalent indicating the location of all
     streams, the location of all proposed and

Our File No. A-91-566
Page 5


existing logging truck roads, and indicating
boundaries of all site I classification
timberlands to be stocked in accordance with
subdivision (b) Section 4561 and any other
site classifications if the board establishes
specific minimum stocking standards for other
site classifications.

(d) A description of the silvicultural
methods to be applied, including the type of
logging equipment to be used.

(e) An outline of the methods to be used
to avoid excessive accelerated erosion from
timber operations to be conducted within the
proximity of a stream.

(f) Special provisions, if any, to
protect any unique area within the area of
timber operations.

(g) The expected dates of commencement
and completion of timber operations.

(h) A certification by the registered
professional forester preparing the plan that
he or she or a designee has personally
inspected the plan area.

(i) Any other information the board
provides by regulation to meet its rules and
the standards of this chapter.

Consequently, Mr. Abraham may prepare drawings or submissions
of an architectural, engineering or similar nature, including
timber harvest plans, to be used by a client in connection with a
proceeding before his own agency.

Necessary Contact with Staff within the Meaning of Regulation
18700.1

Regulation 18700.1 expressly excludes direct oral or written
contact with the agency with regard to the client's proceeding
before the agency except for necessary contact with agency staff
concerning the processing or evaluation of the drawings of
submissions prepared by the official. The provision has been
narrowly interpreted by the Commission to permit the official only
to respond to questions from the staff of the agency concerning
the submissions or plans. (Levinger Advice Letter, No. I-88-328;
West Advice Letter, No. A-88-413.)

Our File No. A-91-566
Page 6


     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                    By: John W. Wallace
                                        Counsel, Legal Division
SH:JWW:aa




3    Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question:
1. May Mr. Abraham, a member of the California Regional Water Quality Control Board, prepare drawings or submissions regarding Timber Harvest Plans to be used by a client in connection with a proceeding before the California Regional Water Quality Control Board?

2. To what extent is Mr. Abraham permitted to contact staff of the California Regional Water Quality Control Board with regard to the client’s proceeding before the agency?

, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2. The plan generally requires considerable experience in on-site studies and engineering work. In preparing the plan, the professional forester uses his knowledge of silviculture, forest soils, and other influences (such as fire control erosion and water quality). Where more than nominal damage is expected, measures to minimize and mitigate such damage, if not already included in the plan by the professional forester, are suggested and discussed to a conclusion by those attending.

2. To what extent is Mr. Abraham permitted to contact staff of the California Regional Water Quality Control Board with regard to the client’s proceeding before the agency?

CONCLUSION: 1. Mr. Abraham may prepare drawings or submissions regarding timber harvest plans to be used by a client in connection with a proceeding before the California Regional Water Quality Control Board.

, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2. Mr. Abraham is prohibited from direct oral or written contact with the California Regional Water Quality Control Board with regard to the timber harvest plan except for necessary contact with agency staff. "Necessary contact" has been interpreted to permit the official only to respond to questions from the staff of the agency concerning the submissions or plans.
